title: Test Qamar Arc Package
description: A larger import test package with multiple characters, one institution, one story arc, one event, and optional cover art.

characters.title: Sheikha Qamar al-Zahra
characters.summary: A major sheikha at the center of this test story arc.
characters.body: Sheikha Qamar al-Zahra stands as a measured, luminous authority in this test package. Her role here is to anchor the arc and drive the relational graph across the imported bundle.

relations.notes: Primary institution in the test arc.

media.alt: Cover art for Sheikha Qamar al-Zahra

characters.title: Sheikha Rahima al-Hanuf
characters.summary: A supporting sheikha used to verify multi-character relational imports.
characters.body: Sheikha Rahima al-Hanuf enters the arc as the stabilizing and interpretive counterpart to Qamar, helping establish a broader canonical network.

relations.notes: Shared institution for the test arc.
Appears in the main arc story.

characters.title: Iyed al-Qasr
characters.summary: A supporting witness-character used to test a three-character arc.
characters.body: Iyed al-Qasr provides an outside perspective for the story arc, helping verify that the importer handles more than one character chain cleanly.

stories.title: The Morning Council of Qamar
stories.summary: A story arc bringing together Qamar, Rahima, and Iyed in a ceremonial test scenario.
stories.body:
This story arc exists to verify that the importer can handle a larger canonical bundle with multiple characters, linked lore, and a coherent narrative unit.

The morning council marks a symbolic gathering, where the arc unfolds through discussion, observation, and ritual continuity. It is intentionally written as a test story rather than a permanent canon chapter.

media.alt: Story cover image for The Morning Council of Qamar

lore.title: The Sanctum of Guardian Arts
lore.summary: A supporting institution for the test arc.
lore.body: The Sanctum of Guardian Arts serves as the narrative and administrative center of the test package. It is deliberately designed to support multiple characters and a single arc.

lore.title: Custodial Stillness
lore.summary: A small doctrine entry used to extend the package beyond just people and story.
lore.body: Custodial Stillness is a conceptual test entry added to verify that lore can sit beside the story arc without breaking the import pipeline.

timeline.title: The Qamar Council Proclamation
timeline.summary: A timeline event that anchors the story arc in a specific historical moment.
timeline.body: The proclamation is the formal event that frames the test arc and verifies that event entries import correctly alongside characters and lore.

media.title: Qamar Arc Cover
media.summary: Cover artwork for the Qamar test arc.
media.alt: Cover image for The Morning Council of Qamar
